chapter: Chapter: ROLE OF THE GOVERNMENT IN HEALTH

questions.question: What is the primary responsibility of the government in a democracy?
questions.answer: To work for the welfare of the people.

questions.question: What does 'Health' mean according to the chapter?
questions.answer: Health means our ability to remain free of illness and injuries.

questions.question: Apart from disease, what else affects our health?
questions.answer: Clean drinking water, pollution-free environment, and adequate food.

questions.question: What are public health services?
questions.answer: A chain of health centres and hospitals run by the government.

questions.question: What are private health services?
questions.answer: Health facilities that are owned or controlled by individuals or companies, not the government.

questions.question: Who is the 'Health Minister' at the state level?
questions.answer: The elected representative responsible for the health department of the state.

questions.question: Name one major water-borne disease mentioned in the text.
questions.answer: Diarrhoea (or worms/hepatitis).

questions.question: What is the full form of PHC?
questions.answer: Primary Health Centre.

questions.question: What are RMPs?
questions.answer: Registered Medical Practitioners.

questions.question: From where does the government get money for public health services?
questions.answer: From the taxes paid by the public.

questions.question: Which state in India took a major step in 1996 by giving 40% of the budget to Panchayats?
questions.answer: Kerala

questions.question: Which country is considered to be one of the healthiest in Central America?
questions.answer: Costa Rica

questions.question: What was the main reason for Costa Rica's health success?
questions.answer: The government decided not to have an army and spend that money on health and education.

questions.question: Are private hospitals usually cheap or expensive?
questions.answer: Expensive

questions.question: Which character in the story had to spend a lot of money in a private hospital?
questions.answer: Aman

questions.question: Who had to wait in long queues at the government hospital in the story?
questions.answer: Ranjan

questions.question: What is the role of a Village Health Worker?
questions.answer: To deal with common illnesses and work under the supervision of doctors at PHCs.

questions.question: What does 'Public' in public health service mean?
questions.answer: It means the service is meant for all citizens of the country.

questions.question: Which fundamental right does the Constitution link to health services?
questions.answer: Right to Life.

questions.question: What is one way private services try to increase profit?
questions.answer: By prescribing unnecessary medicines or tests.

questions.question: Do poor people in India get adequate healthcare?
questions.answer: No, they often face difficulties due to cost and lack of nearby facilities.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Mobile Clinics?
questions.answer: To provide medical facilities to remote or rural areas where hospitals are far away.

questions.question: Can private companies own hospitals in cities?
questions.answer: Yes, many large hospitals in cities are owned by private corporations.

questions.question: Is India one of the largest producers of doctors in the world?
questions.answer: Yes.

questions.question: Why is 'Communicable Disease' a major concern for the government?
questions.answer: Because they spread from one person to another through water, food, or air.
